metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In the last AC bullet, replace the reference to 'the literal fragment that S5 wrote' with the exact current string(s) present in `docs/config.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`, and `session_id.py` that the test must assert absence of (tool-verify these files and quote them inline).
findings.finding: The AC for vocabulary-alignment testing references 'the literal `claude-code_<ver>` / `opencode` fragment that S5 wrote' without quoting the exact string those three files currently contain. A test implementer who hasn't read S5's output must guess the literal bytes to assert absence of — making the AC under-specified as a processing rule.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — last bullet ('The `claude-code_<ver>` vocabulary examples…')

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit mention in the 'Vocabulary alignment' bullet (What section) that the three target files also contain `opencode` vocabulary examples (written by S5) that must likewise be updated to base-name + optional `_<version>` form, so the AC7 proof step is fully grounded in the described work.
findings.finding: AC7's proof step references 'the literal `claude-code_<ver>` / `opencode` fragment that S5 wrote' as the string the test must assert is ABSENT from all three vocabulary files, but the What section's vocabulary-alignment description mentions only updating `claude-code_<ver>` examples — it never mentions an `opencode` fragment or that the three files contain opencode vocabulary examples that also need changing. An implementer following the What would not know to handle the opencode string, yet the AC7 test will assert on it.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — final criterion (vocabulary alignment); What — 'Vocabulary alignment' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section's 'Vocabulary alignment' bullet, after 'Update the `claude-code_<ver>` examples in `docs/config.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`, and the `session_id.py` comment', add: '(These files also contain an `opencode` harness-tag example from S5 that should be updated to the same base-name + optional `_<version>` form for consistency.)' This aligns the description with what AC7's test actually asserts.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a stderr log line (e.g., `echo >&2 'claude-code-session-start: …'`) for each distinct silent-failure path (unwritable file, missing python3, blank session_id when CLAUDE_ENV_FILE IS set) so that Claude Code's hook stderr capture — or the operator's terminal — surfaces the failure automatically, not only via manual verification.
findings.finding: Silent no-op failures produce no observable signal at runtime: when the hook silently fails due to an unwritable CLAUDE_ENV_FILE directory, an absent python3, or a future env-mechanism mismatch, the script exits 0 with no stderr output, leaving operators with only a manual post-hoc verification procedure and no automated signal that the hook is not functioning.
findings.location: What section (graceful degradation paragraph); AC: 'Robust no-op (exit 0, never blocks the session)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the shell script, emit a one-line diagnostic to stderr for each non-trivial no-op (e.g., `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is set but the file is unwritable, or `session_id` is blank despite a parseable JSON envelope). Purely absent `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` can stay silent (intentional opt-out), but actionable misconfigurations should log to stderr. Update the AC to assert these stderr lines appear in the corresponding test cases.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the strip-then-rewrite operation atomic — either use a file lock (e.g., `flock` in shell or `fcntl` in the python3 parsing step) or write to a temp file and `os.replace()` it into place — and add a concurrent-fire test case to `test_session_shims.py` that asserts the invariant holds when two invocations race.
findings.finding: The strip-then-rewrite idempotency pattern on CLAUDE_ENV_FILE is not atomic, creating a TOCTOU race when multiple SessionStart hooks fire concurrently (e.g., subagents), which the plan itself acknowledges as a real scenario under gotcha G1.
findings.location: What section (idempotent re-fire paragraph); AC: 'Idempotent re-fire'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Python parsing/writing block, open the env file with an exclusive `flock` (or use Python's `fcntl.flock(fd, fcntl.LOCK_EX)`) before reading and release after writing. Alternatively, write the final content to a `.tmp` sibling and use `os.replace()` for an atomic swap. Update the AC idempotency bullet to explicitly cover concurrent-fire safety.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete pointer (screenshot, CLI `--help` output excerpt, or version-pinned doc quote) that confirms `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is exactly the variable name used by Claude Code's hook-to-session env-injection mechanism, so the central integration assumption is verifiably grounded rather than citation-only.
findings.finding: The entire env-injection mechanism rests on `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` being the correct Claude Code CLI env-injection variable name, but this string appears zero times anywhere in the repo (src/, docs/, tests/, scripts/). The plan cites an external URL (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks) as authority, but that URL cannot be verified by repo tools. If the actual Claude Code CLI uses a different variable name (e.g. `CLAUDE_HOOK_ENV_FILE` or a file-path passed differently), the hook silently no-ops for all users with no observable failure — precisely the failure mode the plan's verification procedure is meant to surface.
findings.location: What section — Hook script description: 'when `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is set, APPENDS … to it. These are the Claude Code hook-contract facts … per the **Claude Code hooks reference** (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the script header comment and in `docs/session-id-shims.md`, quote the exact Claude Code CLI version and the relevant `claude hooks` or `claude --help` excerpt that shows `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` as the env-injection variable; pin the minimum CLI version that exposes this contract. This makes the external assumption auditable and version-traceable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify with ticket 7656 (crusted-historic-giraffe) that its 'per-harness install docs' deliverable explicitly targets `docs/session-id-shims.md` as the extension point, or update the OUT-scope exclusion to name a specific coordination contract (e.g. a placeholder section in the new doc that 7656 will fill).
findings.finding: The Scope section's OUT exclusion 'Codex/Cursor shims (S6 / 7656, which extend the same docs page)' asserts that ticket 7656 will extend `docs/session-id-shims.md` (the new page this plan creates). Ticket 7656's actual description says only 'Per-harness install docs' without naming `docs/session-id-shims.md` as the target; its `file_impact` lists `docs/` generically. If 7656's author creates separate per-harness docs pages or appends to a different existing file, the Codex/Cursor install guidance will never appear in `docs/session-id-shims.md`, leaving the new page as a Claude Code-only page with no indication that Codex/Cursor users need a different doc.
findings.location: Scope section — OUT: 'Codex/Cursor shims (S6 / 7656, which extend the same docs page)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a placeholder `## Codex (coming in story 7656)` and `## Cursor (coming in story 7656)` section to `docs/session-id-shims.md` in this plan's scope, so the structural intent is locked in and 7656 has a clear fill-in target rather than a free-form 'per-harness install docs' mandate.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` to the Scope's edit-target list (or explicitly acknowledge it requires no changes and explain why the bare `claude-code` exemplar is not needed there).
findings.finding: `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` is an unenumerated edit target: it reads `docs/config.md` and `docs/event-schema.md` in `test_docs_document_provenance` (lines 191–197) and uses `'claude-code_1.2.3'` as a hardcoded `resolve_harness()` exemplar (line 60–61). The vocabulary-alignment edits the plan makes to those two doc files are consumed by this existing test file, yet it appears nowhere in the Scope section or AC edit list.
findings.location: Scope section / Vocabulary alignment bullet / AC7
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` to the edit targets with a note: update `test_resolve_harness` to also assert `resolve_harness() == 'claude-code'` (bare form) as a valid value, consistent with the new vocabulary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Address the TOCTOU race in the strip-then-rewrite idempotency mechanism: either (a) write exports to a per-invocation temp file then atomically `mv` it to CLAUDE_ENV_FILE, or (b) document that concurrent SessionStart fires (subagents) are out-of-scope for this shim and that last-writer-wins is acceptable, and update AC5 to reflect the actual guarantee.
findings.finding: The idempotent re-fire mechanism (read CLAUDE_ENV_FILE → strip prior REBAR_SESSION_ID/AI_AGENT lines → overwrite) is a non-atomic read-modify-write. The plan explicitly flags subagents and resume/fork/compact as re-fire scenarios (gotcha G1), meaning two concurrent SessionStart invocations are a plausible and described case. If both read the file before either writes, the later write clobbers the earlier strip result and the file can accumulate duplicate export lines or retain a stale id — violating the stated guarantee ('never accumulates duplicate/stale lines and always reflects the CURRENT session'). The AC5 test only exercises sequential fires and does not catch this race.
findings.location: What section — 'Idempotent: re-firing on resume/clear/compact first STRIPS…'; AC5
findings.suggested_fix: Use atomic rename: write the filtered + new-export content to `${CLAUDE_ENV_FILE}.tmp.$$` then `mv -f` it to `${CLAUDE_ENV_FILE}`. On POSIX filesystems this is atomic for same-filesystem renames. Add a note in the docs that concurrent subagent fires converge to one of the concurrent session ids (last mv wins), which is acceptable since each subagent legitimately has a distinct session id anyway.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and snapshot-cite the two load-bearing Claude Code hook-contract facts before implementation: (1) confirm `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is the exact env-var name Claude Code uses to inject hook exports into the session (with append semantics), and (2) confirm `session_id` is the exact field name in the SessionStart stdin JSON envelope — add the confirmed names, a documentation version/date reference (or an archived URL), and a note to the script header so future maintainers can re-verify.
findings.finding: The plan's entire injection mechanism rests on two load-bearing Claude Code CLI capability assertions that are unverified in the repo: (1) that `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is the correct env-var name for hook-to-session env injection (with append-via-`>>` semantics), and (2) that `session_id` is the exact field name in the SessionStart stdin JSON envelope. `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` appears zero times in the codebase (src/, docs/, tests/, scripts/). The sole citation is an unarchived, unversioned URL (`https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks`) that cannot be verified at plan-review time and may drift. The plan's fail-safe (no `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` set → silent no-op) mitigates operational safety but does not resolve the T1 risk: if either name is wrong, the feature silently no-ops on every installation without any diagnostic signal other than the manual verification procedure.
findings.location: What section — 'these are the Claude Code hook-contract facts — the SessionStart stdin `session_id` field and the `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` env-injection file — per the Claude Code hooks reference (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the script header comment, replace the bare URL citation with a versioned/dated reference or an archived snapshot URL (e.g. web.archive.org). Add a one-line `# Verified: CLAUDE_ENV_FILE confirmed in Claude Code docs vX.Y (YYYY-MM-DD)` comment to the script and to `docs/session-id-shims.md`. If the URL is not archivable, add a manual-verification note directly in the AC (e.g. 'Implementer must open the URL and confirm the var name before merging').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **[T3 — Unverified external capability]** Confirm the two Claude Code hook-contract facts — the `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` env-injection var name and the `session_id` field in the SessionStart stdin JSON — by copying a concrete excerpt from the Claude Code hooks reference docs page (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks) into the plan or the script header, or by adding a SPIKE acceptance criterion that runs a real Claude Code session and observes the actual env vars and stdin payload before committing the implementation.
findings.finding: The two load-bearing Claude Code hook-contract facts — (1) `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` as the env-injection variable name and (2) `session_id` as the JSON field name in the SessionStart stdin payload — are cited to a single external URL (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks) that cannot be fetched in this environment and is corroborated by zero evidence in the repo (no src, docs, tests, or scripts file references either name). Both are classified Unverified. If either fact is wrong (e.g. the actual var is named `CLAUDE_HOOK_ENV_FILE`, or the JSON field is `sessionId`), the shim silently no-ops for all users — which is the failure mode the entire feature is trying to prevent.
findings.location: What section — 'Hook script' paragraph; AC2 (env-file output proof); AC3 (no-op proof)
findings.suggested_fix: In the script header, paste the exact relevant excerpt from the Claude Code hooks documentation (the table or paragraph that names `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` and the SessionStart JSON schema) and date-stamp it with the Claude Code version it was observed on. In `docs/session-id-shims.md`, add a note like: 'Verified against Claude Code vX.Y.Z; if you are on a different version, run the manual verification procedure immediately after install.' This turns the unverified external citation into a partially-verified, versioned reference.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an inbound link to `docs/session-id-shims.md` from at least one existing navigation point (e.g., `docs/config.md` 'See also' section, `README.md`, or the docs-site nav manifest) so the new page is discoverable.
findings.finding: The new `docs/session-id-shims.md` page is not confirmed to be linked from any top-level index, README, or navigation structure — only that it cross-links to `docs/config.md` (outbound). A new architectural doc page with no inbound navigation link is effectively orphaned and undiscoverable.
findings.suggested_fix: In `docs/config.md`, add a 'See also: [Session ID shims for local CLI](session-id-shims.md)' reference under the AI_AGENT / harness-tag section. If a mkdocs.yml or equivalent nav manifest exists, add the page there too.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit note in the Scope or AC1 that `scripts/session-shims/` must be created as a new directory (it does not yet exist in the repo).
findings.finding: The plan names `scripts/session-shims/claude-code-session-start.sh` as the primary deliverable but never specifies that the `scripts/session-shims/` directory itself must be created — it does not exist in the repo. An implementer following the plan literally could miss the `mkdir` step.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, prepend: 'The `scripts/session-shims/` directory is new and must be created; then …'
